# Sandbox Policy CI Drift

Repair the repo so local CI, the workflow preview generator, and the
checked-in workflow all use the same Codex sandbox and approval-policy
contract.

Required outcomes:

- Canonical active tokens are `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
- The deprecated input spelling `workspace-write` must still parse from
  config files for compatibility.
- Emitted preview JSON and the workflow file must stay canonical-only.
- Update the release note so operators understand the compatibility rule.

Editable surfaces:

- `codex/policy.py`
- `codex/config.py`
- `scripts/render_workflow_preview.py`
- `scripts/run_ci.py`
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- `codex/config.toml`
- `docs/releases/policy-rename.md`

Immutable surfaces:

- `tests/*`
- `tests/fixtures/*`
- `docs/archive/*`
- `repo_evidence/*`
- `release_context/*`
- `incident_context/*`
- `Makefile`
- `Dockerfile`
- `AGENTS.md`

Final validation command:

- `make ci`

Variant details live in `.scenario_variant`.

items.text: Inspect scenario variant and current contract across parser, config, preview, CI, workflow, and release note
Patch only the allowed files so parsing accepts compatibility input while emitted artifacts stay canonical
Run `make ci`, fix any remaining failures, and verify final state

item.aggregated_output: Baseline: repair parser, preview, CI workflow, config, and release note without dropping the deprecated input alias.

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
from pathlib import Path

from codex.config import load_config
from codex.policy import preview_contract


def render_preview(config_path: str | Path = "codex/config.toml") -> dict[str, object]:
    policy = load_config(config_path)
    preview_policy = preview_contract(policy)
    return {
        "schema_version": "sandbox_preview.v1",
        "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
        "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
        "jobs": [
            {
                "job_id": "policy-smoke",
                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
            },
            {
                "job_id": "preview-render",
                "sandbox": preview_policy["sandbox"],
                "approval_policy": preview_policy["approval_policy"],
            },
        ],
    }


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--config", default="codex/config.toml")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    print(json.dumps(render_preview(args.config), indent=2, sort_keys=True))
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path


def main() -> int:
    env = os.environ.copy()
    visible_fixture = Path("tests/fixtures/visible_config.toml")
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="visible_fixture_") as tmp:
        temp_fixture = Path(tmp) / "visible_config.toml"
        shutil.copy(visible_fixture, temp_fixture)
        patched = temp_fixture.read_text(encoding="utf-8").replace(
            "workspace_write",
            "workspace-write",
        )
        temp_fixture.write_text(patched, encoding="utf-8")
        env["CODEX_CONFIG_FIXTURE"] = str(temp_fixture)
        return subprocess.call([sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  policy-smoke:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: make ci

  preview-render:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: manual-review
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml

[policy]
sandbox = "workspace_write"
approval_policy = "on_request"

# Policy Rename

The repo moved from hyphenated sandbox tokens to underscored names.
This release is fully breaking: old config spellings no longer work.

Preview examples may still display `manual-review` until downstream
automation catches up.

item.text: The divergence is clear already: the parser only accepts deprecated sandbox spellings, preview rewrites `on_request` to `manual-review`, and the workflow preview job is still using the old approval token. I’m reading the tests next so I can patch to the intended contract without touching immutable fixtures.

item.text: The test filenames differ from the obvious guesses, but the grep output is enough to see the target assertions: canonical `workspace_write` and `on_request` everywhere after parsing/rendering. I’m opening the actual test modules now and then patching the allowed files to match that contract.

item.aggregated_output:
from __future__ import annotations

from codex.config import load_config
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_parser_accepts_visible_fixture() -> None:
    loaded = load_config(fixture_path())
    assert loaded["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert loaded["approval_policy"] == "on_request"

from __future__ import annotations

from scripts.render_workflow_preview import render_preview
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_preview_emits_canonical_policy_tokens() -> None:
    preview = render_preview(fixture_path())
    assert preview["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert preview["approval_policy"] == "on_request"
    assert {job["approval_policy"] for job in preview["jobs"]} == {"on_request"}

item.text: I’m editing the contract code now: the parser will accept both sandbox spellings but normalize to canonical tokens, and preview rendering will stop rewriting approval policy names. After that I’ll harden `scripts/run_ci.py` so `make ci` checks the generated preview and the checked-in workflow for canonical-only output.

The core contract patch is in: parser normalization is fixed, preview emission is canonical, the workflow env uses `on_request`, and the release note now explains the compatibility boundary. I’m running `make ci` again to catch any remaining issues, including the new workflow/preview checks in `scripts/run_ci.py`.

`make ci` exposed a separate script-path issue: both repo scripts were relying on imports that only work when the repo root is already on `PYTHONPATH`. I’m fixing those entrypoints so they work from the checked-in command paths used by `make` and the workflow.

item.text:
Repaired the contract so the repo now treats `workspace_write` and `on_request` as the only emitted values, while still accepting `workspace-write` on input. The substantive fixes are in [codex/policy.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run3/workspace/codex/policy.py), [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run3/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py), [scripts/run_ci.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run3/workspace/scripts/run_ci.py), [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run3/workspace/.github/workflows/ci.yml), and [docs/releases/policy-rename.md](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02b_live_probe/workdirs/v1-clean-baseline-run3/workspace/docs/releases/policy-rename.md). `codex/config.toml` was already canonical, and `codex/config.py` already returned normalized config output, so those did not need edits.

`scripts/run_ci.py` now also verifies that the rendered preview JSON and checked-in workflow contain only canonical tokens, and it selects a Python interpreter that actually has `pytest` installed on this machine so `make ci` can run successfully. I did not touch any immutable evidence or tests.

Verification: `make ci` passes, and `python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml` emits only `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
